riddles.riddle: I have a face and two hands, but no arms or legs. What am I?
riddles.explanation: Clocks have a face (the front) and hands (the pointers) but no real arms or legs.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: It tells you what time it is.

riddles.riddle: What has keys but cannot open any locks?
riddles.explanation: Pianos have keys (the white and black parts you press), but they are not for opening doors.

riddles.riddle: I have a tail and a head, but no body. What am I?
riddles.explanation: Coins have a head (the front picture) and a tail (the back side), but no body.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: You use it to buy candy.

riddles.riddle: What gets wetter as it dries?
riddles.explanation: When you use a towel to dry something, the towel gets wet.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: You use it after a bath.

riddles.riddle: What has to be broken before you can use it?
riddles.explanation: You have to crack or break the shell of an egg to cook and eat it.

riddles.riddle: I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I?
riddles.explanation: Maps show places, but they are pictures, not real things.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: It helps you find your way.

riddles.riddle: What has a thumb and four fingers but is not alive?
riddles.explanation: Gloves have shapes for fingers including a thumb, but they are made of fabric.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: You wear it on your hand in winter.

riddles.riddle: What can you catch but never throw?
riddles.explanation: You can catch a cold (get sick), but you cannot throw a cold like a ball.

riddles.riddle: What building has the most stories?
riddles.explanation: 'Stories' can mean tales in books or floors in a building. Libraries have many storybooks.

riddles.riddle: What is full of holes but still holds water?
riddles.explanation: Sponges have many tiny holes, but they soak up and hold water.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: You use it to clean dishes.

riddles.riddle: I am light as a feather, yet the strongest person cannot hold me for five minutes. What am I?
riddles.explanation: Holding your breath for more than a few minutes is impossible for anyone.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: You do this all day without thinking.

riddles.riddle: What belongs to you but other people use it more than you do?
riddles.explanation: Your name is yours, but other people say it more often than you say it yourself.

riddles.riddle: What has words but never speaks?
riddles.explanation: Books have many words, but they cannot talk out loud.

riddles.riddle: What has a bottom at the top?
riddles.explanation: Your legs have bottoms (your feet) at the top of your legs? No - wait! The answer is actually 'legs' because the bottom of your legs (feet) are at the bottom. Many children get this one! Alternative answer: a down feather.

riddles.riddle: What can you hold without ever touching?
riddles.explanation: You can hold your breath without touching anything physical.
riddles.kid_friendly_hint: You do this when you go underwater.
